text:
(a) Farm-to-Market Road 1015 between U.S. Highway 83 and the Progreso International Bridge is designated as the Bill Summers International Boulevard.

(b) Subject to Section 225.021 (General Provisions) (c), the department shall design and construct memorial markers indicating the highway number, the designation as the Bill Summers International Boulevard, and any other appropriate information.

(c) The department shall erect a marker at each end of the boulevard and at appropriate intermediate sites along the boulevard.

(d) The requirement that a recognized individual be deceased under Section 225.001 (Restrictions on Naming Highway) (c) does not apply to this section.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 6
### Chapter 225. State Highway Names
##### § 225.077 — Bill Summers International Boulevard

(a) Farm-to-Market Road 1015 between U.S. Highway 83 and the Progreso International Bridge is designated as the Bill Summers International Boulevard.

(b) Subject to Section 225.021 (General Provisions) (c), the department shall design and construct memorial markers indicating the highway number, the designation as the Bill Summers International Boulevard, and any other appropriate information.

(c) The department shall erect a marker at each end of the boulevard and at appropriate intermediate sites along the boulevard.

(d) The requirement that a recognized individual be deceased under Section 225.001 (Restrictions on Naming Highway) (c) does not apply to this section.

*Added by Acts 2009, 81st Leg., R.S., Ch. 563 (S.B.1997), Sec. 2, eff. June 19, 2009.*
